1. A tool that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI)
2. A prompt template for summarization
3. Logging that doesn't interfere with stdio JSON-RPC protocol

data.content:
Empty directory. I'll scaffold a complete MCP server project. Here's the approach:

1. **Stdio transport** — all JSON-RPC goes over stdout, so logging must go to **stderr** only.
2. **Tool** — `FetchApi` tool that uses `HttpClient` via DI to call an external URL.
3. **Prompt** — `Summarize` prompt template.
4. **DI** — wire up `HttpClient`, logging, and the MCP server using the official `ModelContextProtocol` SDK.

result.content:
1. # MCP C# SDK
2. 
3. [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)
4. 
5. The official C# SDK for the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/), enabling .NET applications, services, and libraries to implement and interact with MCP clients and servers. Please visit the [API documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.
6. 
7. ## Packages
8. 
9. This SDK consists of three main packages:
10. 
11. - **[ModelContextProtocol.Core](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.Core)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.Core.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.Core) - For projects that only need to use the client or low-level server APIs and want the minimum number of dependencies.
12. 
13. - **[ModelContextProtocol](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol) - The main package with hosting and dependency injection extensions. References `ModelContextProtocol.Core`. This is the right fit for most projects that don't need HTTP server capabilities.
14. 
15. - **[ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore) - The library for HTTP-based MCP servers. References `ModelContextProtocol`.
16. 
17. ## Getting Started
18. 
19. To get started, see the [Getting Started](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/concepts/getting-started.html) guide for installation instructions, package-selection guidance, and complete examples for both clients and servers.
20. 
21. You can also browse the [samples](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/csharp-sdk/tree/main/samples) directory and the [API documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.
22. 
23. ## About MCP
24. 
25. The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol that standardizes how applications provide context to Large Language Models (LLMs). It enables secure integration between LLMs and various data sources and tools.
26. 
27. For more information about MCP:
28. 
29. - [Official MCP Documentation](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/)
30. - [MCP C# SDK Documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/)
31. - [Protocol Specification](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/)
32. - [GitHub Organization](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol)
33.

@@ -1,33 +1,33 @@
 # MCP C# SDK
 
 [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)
 
 The official C# SDK for the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/), enabling .NET applications, services, and libraries to implement and interact with MCP clients and servers. Please visit the [API documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.
 
 ## Packages
 
 This SDK consists of three main packages:
 
 - **[ModelContextProtocol.Core](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.Core)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.Core.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.Core) - For projects that only need to use the client or low-level server APIs and want the minimum number of dependencies.
 
 - **[ModelContextProtocol](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol) - The main package with hosting and dependency injection extensions. References `ModelContextProtocol.Core`. This is the right fit for most projects that don't need HTTP server capabilities.
 
 - **[ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore)** [![NuGet version](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.svg)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore) - The library for HTTP-based MCP servers. References `ModelContextProtocol`.
 
 ## Getting Started
 
 To get started, see the [Getting Started](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/concepts/getting-started.html) guide for installation instructions, package-selection guidance, and complete examples for both clients and servers.
 
 You can also browse the [samples](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/csharp-sdk/tree/main/samples) directory and the [API documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/api/ModelContextProtocol.html) for more details on available functionality.
 
 ## About MCP
 
 The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol that standardizes how applications provide context to Large Language Models (LLMs). It enables secure integration between LLMs and various data sources and tools.
 
 For more information about MCP:
 
 - [Official MCP Documentation](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/)
 - [MCP C# SDK Documentation](https://csharp.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/)
 - [Protocol Specification](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/)
 - [GitHub Organization](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol)

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

xml_file = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml'
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

# Find all members and print the ones related to attributes and key types
target_patterns = [
    'McpServerToolAttribute',
    'McpServerPromptAttribute',
    'McpServerResourceAttribute',
    'McpServerTool',
    'McpServerPrompt',
    'McpServerResource',
    'WithStdioServerTransport',
    'AddMcpServer',
    'IMcpServerBuilder'
]

members_found = {}

for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    for pattern in target_patterns:
        if pattern in name:
            members_found[name] = member
            break

# Print details about each found member
for name in sorted(members_found.keys()):
    member = members_found[name]
    print(f"\n{'='*80}")
    print(f"NAME: {name}")
    print(f"{'='*80}")
    
    for child in member:
        tag = child.tag.split('}')[-1]  # Remove namespace
        if tag == 'summary':
            text = ''.join(child.itertext()).strip()
            print(f"SUMMARY:\n{text}\n")
        elif tag == 'param':
            param_name = child.get('name')
            param_text = ''.join(child.itertext()).strip()
            print(f"PARAM {param_name}: {param_text}")
        elif tag == 'returns':
            returns_text = ''.join(child.itertext()).strip()
            print(f"RETURNS: {returns_text}")

EOF

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import re

xml_file = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml'
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

# Look for type definitions (T: prefix)
print("=" * 80)
print("TYPE DEFINITIONS")
print("=" * 80)

for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    
    # Type definitions
    if name.startswith('T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer'):
        short_name = name.split('.')[-1]
        print(f"\nTYPE: {short_name}")
        
        summary_elem = member.find('{*}summary')
        if summary_elem is not None:
            summary = ''.join(summary_elem.itertext()).strip()[:200]
            print(f"  {summary}")

# Look for method signatures on extensions
print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("EXTENSION METHODS")
print("=" * 80)

for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    
    if 'McpServerBuilderExtensions' in name and name.startswith('M:'):
        # Extract method name
        match = re.search(r'\.(\w+)\(', name)
        if match:
            method_name = match.group(1)
            print(f"\nMETHOD: {method_name}")
            print(f"  Full: {name}")
            
            summary_elem = member.find('{*}summary')
            if summary_elem is not None:
                summary = ''.join(summary_elem.itertext()).strip()[:150]
                print(f"  Summary: {summary}")

EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

xml_file = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml'
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

# Find McpServerTool and McpServerPrompt types
print("=" * 80)
print("CORE TYPES FOR TOOLS AND PROMPTS")
print("=" * 80)

target_types = [
    'McpServerTool',
    'McpServerPrompt', 
    'McpServerResource',
    'McpToolResult',
    'McpPromptResult'
]

for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    
    if name.startswith('T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.'):
        short_name = name.split('.')[-1]
        if any(target in short_name for target in target_types):
            print(f"\nTYPE: {short_name}")
            print(f"  Full: {name}")
            
            summary_elem = member.find('{*}summary')
            if summary_elem is not None:
                summary = ''.join(summary_elem.itertext()).strip()
                if summary:
                    print(f"  Summary: {summary}")

EOF

xml_file = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml'
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

print("=" * 80)
print("RESULT TYPES (CallToolResult, GetPromptResult, etc)")
print("=" * 80)

for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    
    # Look for result types in Protocol namespace
    if 'CallToolResult' in name or 'GetPromptResult' in name or 'TextContent' in name or 'ImageContent' in name:
        short = name.split('.')[-1]
        if name.startswith('T:'):
            print(f"\nTYPE: {short}")
            print(f"  {name}")
            
            summary_elem = member.find('{*}summary')
            if summary_elem is not None:
                summary = ''.join(summary_elem.itertext()).strip()
                if summary:
                    print(f"  {summary[:120]}")

EOF

EOF

import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

# Main package for invoke methods
xml_file = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml'
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

print("=" * 80)
print("INVOCATION METHODS AND RETURN TYPES")
print("=" * 80)

# Look for InvokeAsync methods
for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    
    if 'InvokeAsync' in name and ('McpServerTool' in name or 'McpServerPrompt' in name):
        print(f"\n{name}")
        
        summary_elem = member.find('{*}summary')
        if summary_elem is not None:
            summary = ''.join(summary_elem.itertext()).strip()
            if summary:
                print(f"  Summary: {summary}")
        
        # Get return type
        returns = member.find('{*}returns')
        if returns is not None:
            returns_text = ''.join(returns.itertext()).strip()
            print(f"  Returns: {returns_text}")

EOF

  Represents the result of a tasks/get request.

  Represents the result of a  request sent to the server during connection establishment.

python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

xml_file = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml'
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

print("=" * 80)
print("MCPSERVERPROMPT TYPE AND METHODS")
print("=" * 80)

for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    
    # Look at prompt type specifically
    if ('McpServerPrompt' in name and not 'Type' in name and not 'Attribute' in name and not 'Create' in name 
        and not 'Delegating' in name and not 'AIFunction' in name):
        if name.startswith('M:') or name.startswith('P:'):
            print(f"\n{name}")
            
            summary_elem = member.find('{*}summary')
            if summary_elem is not None:
                summary = ''.join(summary_elem.itertext()).strip()[:100]
                if summary:
                    print(f"  {summary}")
            
            returns = member.find('{*}returns')
            if returns is not None:
                returns_text = ''.join(returns.itertext()).strip()[:100]
                print(f"  Returns: {returns_text}")

EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

xml_file = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml'
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

print("=" * 80)
print("ATTRIBUTE PROPERTY DEFINITIONS")
print("=" * 80)

# Focus on the attribute properties
for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    
    if 'McpServerToolAttribute' in name or 'McpServerPromptAttribute' in name:
        if name.startswith('P:'):
            print(f"\n{name}")
            
            summary_elem = member.find('{*}summary')
            if summary_elem is not None:
                summary = ''.join(summary_elem.itertext()).strip()
                if summary:
                    print(f"  {summary}")
        elif name.startswith('M:') and '#ctor' in name:
            print(f"\nCONSTRUCTOR: {name}")
            
            summary_elem = member.find('{*}summary')
            if summary_elem is not None:
                summary = ''.join(summary_elem.itertext()).strip()
                if summary:
                    print(f"  {summary}")
            
            for param in member.findall('{*}param'):
                param_name = param.get('name')
                param_text = ''.join(param.itertext()).strip()
                print(f"  Param {param_name}: {param_text}")

EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

xml_file = '/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.xml'
tree = ET.parse(xml_file)
root = tree.getroot()

print("=" * 80)
print("LOGGING CONFIGURATION")
print("=" * 80)

for member in root.findall('.//member'):
    name = member.get('name', '')
    
    if any(x in name.lower() for x in ['logging', 'logger', 'stderr', 'loglevel']):
        print(f"\n{name}")
        
        summary_elem = member.find('{*}summary')
        if summary_elem is not None:
            summary = ''.join(summary_elem.itertext()).strip()[:150]
            if summary:
                print(f"  {summary}")

EOF

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me provide a comprehensive answer based on all my discoveries.

## ModelContextProtocol NuGet Package v1.2.0 - Public API Discovery

Based on exploration of the restored package DLLs at `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0`, here's the complete public API for registering MCP stdio servers:

---

### 1. **Server Registration Extension Methods**

**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection`

**Class:** `McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions`
- **`AddMcpServer(IServiceCollection services, Action<McpServerOptions> configureOptions)`** 
  - Adds the MCP server to the service collection with default options

**Class:** `McpServerBuilderExtensions`
- Returns an `IMcpServerBuilder` interface for fluent configuration
- **Key methods:**
  - `WithStdioServerTransport(IMcpServerBuilder builder)` – Configures stdin/stdout transport
  - `WithStreamServerTransport(IMcpServerBuilder builder, Stream inputStream, Stream outputStream)` – Configures custom stream transport
  - `WithTools<TToolType>(IMcpServerBuilder builder, JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions)` – Generic registration of tool types
  - `WithTools<TToolType>(IMcpServerBuilder builder, TToolType target, JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions)` – Register from instance
  - `WithTools(IMcpServerBuilder builder, IEnumerable<McpServerTool> tools)` – Register pre-built tools
  - `WithTools(IMcpServerBuilder builder, IEnumerable<Type> toolTypes, JsonSerializerOptions options)` – Register from multiple types
  - `WithToolsFromAssembly(IMcpServerBuilder builder, Assembly assembly, JsonSerializerOptions options)` – Auto-discovery from assembly
  - Similar methods exist for **`WithPrompts*`** and **`WithResources*`**
  - `WithSetLoggingLevelHandler(IMcpServerBuilder builder, McpRequestHandler<SetLevelRequestParams, EmptyResult> handler)` – Configure logging level handler

---

### 2. **Attributes for Declaring Tools, Prompts, and Resources**

**Namespace:** `ModelContextProtocol.Server`

#### **McpServerToolAttribute**
Applied to methods to expose them as MCP tools:
```csharp
[McpServerTool]
public async Task<CallToolResult> MyMethod(...) { }
```

**Constructor:** `McpServerToolAttribute()` (parameterless)

**Properties (all settable):**
- `string Name` { get; } – Name derived from method name
- `string Title` { get; set; } – Human-readable title for display
- `bool Destructive` { get; set; } – Whether tool may perform destructive updates
- `bool Idempotent` { get; set; } – Whether repeated calls have no additional effect
- `bool OpenWorld` { get; set; } – Whether tool interacts with "open world" external entities
- `bool ReadOnly` { get; set; } – Whether tool does not modify environment
- `bool UseStructuredContent` { get; set; } – Whether tool reports output schema
- `Type OutputSchemaType` { get; set; } – Type from which to generate output schema
- `string IconSource` { get; set; } – URI for tool's icon
- `TaskSupport` { get; set; } – Task execution hints for the tool

#### **McpServerPromptAttribute**
Applied to methods to expose them as MCP prompts:
```csharp
[McpServerPrompt]
public async Task<GetPromptResult> MyPrompt(...) { }
```

**Constructor:** `McpServerPromptAttribute()` (parameterless)

**Properties (all settable):**
- `string Name` { get; set; } – Name of the prompt
- `string Title` { get; set; } – Title of the prompt
- `string IconSource` { get; set; } – URI for prompt's icon

#### **McpServerResourceAttribute**
Applied to methods/properties to expose them as MCP resources:

**Constructor:** `McpServerResourceAttribute()` (parameterless)

**Properties (all settable):**
- `string UriTemplate` { get; set; } – URI template of the resource
- `string Name` { get; set; } – Name of the resource
- `string Title` { get; set; } – Title of the resource
- `string MimeType` { get; set; } – MIME type of the resource
- `string IconSource` { get; set; } – URI for resource's icon

#### **Type-Level Attributes (for class-level marking)**
- **`McpServerToolTypeAttribute`** – Mark a class containing methods with `[McpServerTool]`
- **`McpServerPromptTypeAttribute`** – Mark a class containing methods with `[McpServerPrompt]`
- **`McpServerResourceTypeAttribute`** – Mark a class containing members with `[McpServerResource]`

#### **McpMetaAttribute**
Adds protocol-level metadata to tools/prompts/resources. Multiple instances can be applied:
```csharp
[McpServerTool]
[McpMeta("key", "string_value")]
[McpMeta("number", 42.0)]
[McpMeta("flag", true)]
public async Task<CallToolResult> MyTool(...) { }
```

**Constructors:**
- `McpMetaAttribute(string name, string value)` – String metadata
- `McpMetaAttribute(string name, double value)` – Numeric metadata
